This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Stigmaphyllon sagittatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
